_comment: A realistic MCP server: a competent engineer's first draft, not a strawman. It scores 58 (grade D), between the vague example (14) and the contract-grade rewrite (100). The descriptions read fine to a human but miss the agent-contract subtleties that cause misfires: a money-moving tool with no idempotency contract and a secret passed as a model-visible argument, a tool that forwards raw SQL, and two search tools the model cannot choose between. This is the example to study, because real servers look like this.

tools.name: search_tickets
tools.description: Search the support ticket database by keyword and status. Returns tickets matching the query so the agent can find the relevant conversation before acting.

query.description: Keywords to search ticket subjects and bodies for.

tools.name: find_tickets
tools.description: Look up support tickets in the database using a search term and an optional status filter. Returns the tickets that match so the agent can locate the right conversation.

tools.name: get_customer
tools.description: Fetch a customer record by customer id. Returns the customer's profile, plan tier, and lifetime value as JSON. Returns an error object with the string 'not_found' if no customer has that id.

customer_id.description: The customer's unique id, formatted as 'cus_' followed by 14 alphanumerics, e.g. 'cus_0a1b2c3d4e5f6g'.

tools.name: issue_refund
tools.description: Issues a refund to the customer for the given order.

api_key.description: The Stripe secret key used to authorize the refund.

tools.name: run_analytics_query
tools.description: Runs a SQL query against the analytics warehouse and returns the result rows so the agent can answer reporting questions. Pass any SQL string and it executes against the read replica.

sql.description: The raw SQL query to execute.
